mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production indicates expanding supply capacity, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with INDPRO changes over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses 5.5%, 20-day forward return is not negative in at least 60% of occurrences over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%) combined with a federal funds rate that is already elevated (>0.5%) suggests real interest rates are increasing, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal fails to predict negative returns with rank IC < 0.05 over a 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic expansion (real GDP growth > 3% YoY), a sustained increase in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that drives earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during expansions (GDPC1 YoY > 3%), 20-day forward return correlation with RSAFS changes is not positive over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp one-month drop in industrial production of more than 1% signals imminent supply disruptions and earnings downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns after trigger are not negative in more than 70% of cases over a 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the federal funds rate of 25 basis points or more in a single month signals an unexpected tightening, which increases discount rates and lowers equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns after trigger are positive in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in payroll employment over 3 months signals weakening labor demand, foreshadowing lower consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are not below median in the 6 months following trigger compared to 6 months before, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts of more than 5% month-over-month indicates robust construction activity and consumer confidence, which boosts economic growth and equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns after trigger are negative in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year window, reject.
